west_president_ev_001.title: Wave of National Unity
west_president_ev_001.body: After several tense days, the national mood is finally calming down a little.

west_president_ev_002.title: Market Confidence
west_president_ev_002.body: Investors are coming back with their reassuring charts and their slightly too-white smiles. The economy clearly likes your direction.

west_president_ev_003.title: National Security Exercise
west_president_ev_003.body: The nation's forces are carrying out maneuvers with flawless seriousness. For once, even the uniforms seem happy.

west_president_ev_004.title: Handshake Week
west_president_ev_004.body: Chanceries are opening up, calls are multiplying, and your schedule is starting to look like a permanent summit.

west_president_ev_005.title: Opinion Fatigue
west_president_ev_005.body: The public is sighing faster than usual. Every announcement triggers a nationwide shrug. That is a bad sign.

west_president_ev_006.title: Price Pressure
west_president_ev_006.body: Bills are rising, shopkeepers are getting worried, and even ministers can see the budget deteriorating.

west_president_ev_007.title: Military Supply Delays
west_president_ev_007.body: Essential deliveries are running late. Officially, everything is under control. Unofficially, someone is sweating a lot.

west_president_ev_008.title: Diplomatic Chill
west_president_ev_008.body: Partners are responding slowly, smiles are shorter, and official statements are starting to sting a little.

west_president_ev_009.title: A Speech That Hits the Mark
west_president_ev_009.body: Your address lands at exactly the right moment, with exactly the right words. The country feels understood, which is already almost an institutional miracle.

west_president_ev_010.title: Export Boom
west_president_ev_010.body: Ports are running at full speed, contracts keep piling up, and industrial leaders are starting to speak loudly at dinner again.

west_president_ev_011.title: Threat Handled Well
west_president_ev_011.body: A threat was detected early and handled efficiently, with no major incident.

west_president_ev_012.title: Reconciliation Summit
west_president_ev_012.body: Rivals agree to sit at the same table. Nobody really likes each other, but everyone makes the effort.

west_president_ev_013.title: Presidential Memes
west_president_ev_013.body: An awkward moment is spreading everywhere. The country laughs, the opposition celebrates, and your communications team is having a rough night.

west_president_ev_014.title: Logistics Strike
west_president_ev_014.body: Flows are slowing down, costs are rising, and every warehouse suddenly becomes a national issue. The economy hates this kind of theater.

west_president_ev_015.title: Worn-Out Equipment
west_president_ev_015.body: Experts are talking about delayed maintenance in very polite terms. In plain words, a lot of things should have been repaired yesterday.

west_president_ev_016.title: An Ambassador Too Honest
west_president_ev_016.body: A diplomat says out loud what is normally kept for the hallways. Foreign capitals rarely appreciate raw honesty.

west_president_ev_017.title: Unexpected Popularity
west_president_ev_017.body: A decision considered risky is ultimately applauded. Sometimes the country likes being surprised when it works.

west_president_ev_018.title: Brilliant Tourist Season
west_president_ev_018.body: Visitors are pouring in, hotels are fully booked, and even ministers are suddenly talking about heritage with emotion.

west_president_ev_019.title: Doctrine Clarified
west_president_ev_019.body: The army, the services, and the general staff finally know what line to follow. Consistency remains underrated in politics.

west_president_ev_020.title: Cascade of Agreements
west_president_ev_020.body: Trade, security, culture, research. Everyone wants to sign something with you this week. It is almost suspicious.

west_president_ev_021.title: Diffuse Social Anger
west_president_ev_021.body: It is not one big explosion. It is worse. A buildup of weariness, grumbling, and looks from people who no longer believe in anything.

west_president_ev_022.title: Economic Confidence Breakdown
west_president_ev_022.body: Projects are slowing, hiring is hesitating, and forecasts look like well-dressed excuses.

west_president_ev_023.title: Leak in the Procedures
west_president_ev_023.body: A document is circulating, a method is being commented on live, and suddenly national security sounds very talkative.
west_president_ev_023.effect: Effect: the card message appears without vowels

west_president_ev_024.title: War of Little Words
west_president_ev_024.body: Sharp remarks are piling up between capitals. Nothing dramatic. Just enough to make every call painful.
west_president_ev_024.effect: Effect: the card message appears without vowels

west_president_ev_025.title: Popular Gathering
west_president_ev_025.body: The country gets a little of its breath back. Crowds gather without panic, without scandal, simply with the desire to believe again.
west_president_ev_025.effect: Effect: the choices appear without vowels

west_president_ev_026.title: Industrial Recovery
west_president_ev_026.body: Assembly lines restart, factories hire, and the word sovereignty suddenly becomes profitable again.
west_president_ev_026.effect: Effect: the choices appear without vowels

west_president_ev_027.title: Borders Under Control
west_president_ev_027.body: Surveillance tightens, incidents decrease, and the morning reports finally stop causing nervous tics.
west_president_ev_027.effect: Effect: the card message appears reversed

west_president_ev_028.title: International Easing
west_president_ev_028.body: Tensions are going down, tones are becoming more measured, and your foreign ministry is finally sleeping a little again.
west_president_ev_028.effect: Effect: the card message appears reversed

west_president_ev_029.title: Botched Press Conference
west_president_ev_029.body: One wrong sentence, one badly read look, and everyone decides you meant the worst. Politics is a cruel sport.

west_president_ev_030.title: External Trade Shock
west_president_ev_030.body: One partner slows down, another imposes taxes, a third hesitates. In just a few days, the economic machine starts coughing badly.
